prevalence.trend: increasing with obesity, hypertension, and CKD prevalence

vulnerablePopulations: patients with CKD or metabolic syndrome
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical acute monoarthritis with hyperuricemia; gold standard is monosodium urate crystals in synovial fluid; imaging (ultrasound, DECT) supportive; aligned with ACR/EULAR approaches

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; detected in symptomatic cases
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; urate-lowering therapy adherence and follow-up vary

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care diagnosis common; arthrocentesis and imaging limited outside specialized centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acute flare: NSAIDs/colchicine/corticosteroids; chronic: allopurinol (titrated) with treat-to-target urate, lifestyle counseling; prophylaxis during ULT initiation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to crystal confirmation and variable ULT titration/follow-up in APS
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Core medications (colchicine, allopurinol) are widely available and affordable
